raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint to request an immediate reversal of a charge in the amount of {$1000.00} for a transaction that occurred on XX/XX/year>, for a set of chairs purchased via XXXX XXXX. Upon delivery on XX/XX/year>, we attempted to assemble the product, but the item was immediately found to be defective and unsafe ; specifically, the back support cracked upon the slightest pressure due to poor construction, rendering the item functionally useless and dangerous to sit in. On XX/XX/year>, I contacted the seller to return the unsafe item and was instructed to return the goods to a XXXX store ; however, when I attempted to return the item to XXXX in person, they refused the return and stated I must deal with the seller directly. 

Following this refusal, I reached back out to the seller on XX/XX/year>, but the seller ceased all communication and has not responded since, effectively " ghosting '' me to avoid processing the refund. Consequently, I filed my initial dispute with Citibank on XX/XX/year>, which was well within the protected XXXX timeframe required by the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). Despite these facts, Citibank has failed to protect my consumer rights and has denied my disputes even after receiving blatant evidence of a defective and unsafe product. 

Citibank has not provided specific reasoning for the denial other than stating it is the " customer 's responsibility, '' which demonstrates laziness and a failure to investigate the dispute with due diligence regarding the merchant 's refusal to communicate. I still possess the defective merchandise and remain willing to return it if the merchant provides a prepaid shipping label, which they have failed to do. Currently, Citibank has opened a third and " final '' investigation and has stated the case will be closed if it is denied again ; therefore, I am seeking assistance because the bank 's denial suggests a complete lack of thorough investigation into the merchant 's failure to accept a return of hazardous goods.
